core.label: Elixir Fundamentals
core.description: Syntax, types, pattern matching, and IEx.

topics.label: Installation & IEx
topics.description: Install Elixir and explore with the IEx interactive shell. Elixir runs on the Erlang VM (BEAM).

topics.label: Basic Types
topics.description: atoms, integers, floats, booleans, strings, lists, tuples, maps. Atoms are named constants.

topics.label: Pattern Matching
topics.description: The = operator is match, not assignment. Pattern matching decomposes data everywhere in Elixir.

topics.label: Control Flow
topics.description: case, cond, if/else, with. Prefer pattern matching over if/else.

topics.label: Functions & Modules
topics.description: Named functions in modules (def/defp) and anonymous functions (fn). Arity matters in Elixir.

topics.label: Pipe Operator |>
topics.description: Chain function calls with the pipe. Pipes make data transformation pipelines readable.

topics.label: Lists & Keyword Lists
topics.description: Linked lists with head|tail, keyword lists for options. Lists are the primary sequence type.

topics.label: Maps & Structs
topics.description: Maps for key-value data, structs for typed maps. Structs are named maps with compile-time guarantees.

topics.label: Enum & Stream
topics.description: Enum for eager collection operations, Stream for lazy evaluation. The core data processing modules.

topics.label: Recursion & Tail Calls
topics.description: Elixir has no loops — use recursion with tail-call optimization. Enum abstracts most recursion.

topics.label: Processes
topics.description: Lightweight BEAM processes with spawn, send, receive. Elixir processes are not OS threads.

topics.label: GenServer
topics.description: Generic server behavior for stateful processes. GenServer is the backbone of Elixir applications.

topics.label: Supervisors
topics.description: Monitor and restart child processes. Supervisors implement the 'let it crash' philosophy.

topics.label: Supervision Trees
topics.description: Hierarchical process supervision for fault-tolerant applications. OTP's killer feature.

topics.label: Agent & Task
topics.description: Agent for simple state, Task for async computations. Higher-level abstractions over processes.

topics.label: Error Handling
topics.description: try/rescue/after, throw/catch, and the 'let it crash' approach. Supervisors handle process failures.

topics.label: Mix & Hex
topics.description: Build projects with Mix and manage dependencies with Hex. mix new creates project scaffolding.

topics.label: ExUnit Testing
topics.description: Write tests with ExUnit — assert, refute, setup, describe. Tests run concurrently by default.

topics.label: Protocols
topics.description: Polymorphism with protocols — dispatch on data type. Elixir's answer to type classes.

topics.label: Behaviours
topics.description: Define contracts with @callback. Behaviours are Elixir's interface mechanism.

topics.label: Typespecs & Dialyzer
topics.description: @spec and @type for documentation and static analysis with Dialyzer.

topics.label: Macros
topics.description: Metaprogramming with quote and unquote. Macros transform AST at compile time.

topics.label: Comprehensions
topics.description: for with generators, filters, and into. Elixir comprehensions are more powerful than list comprehensions.

topics.label: Sigils
topics.description: ~r for regex, ~w for word lists, ~s for strings. Sigils provide alternative literal syntax.

topics.label: ETS & DETS
topics.description: In-memory (ETS) and disk (DETS) key-value storage built into the BEAM. Extremely fast lookups.

topics.label: Erlang Interop
topics.description: Call Erlang modules directly from Elixir. Full interoperability with the Erlang ecosystem.
